member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics
member.summary: Provides valid attributes for describing a portable executable file.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.RelocsStripped
member.summary: Indicates that the file does not contain base relocations and must therefore be loaded at its preferred base address.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.Image
member.summary: Indicates that the image file is valid and can be run. If this flag is not set, it indicates a linker error.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.LineNumsStripped
member.summary: Indicates COFF line numbers have been removed. This flag is deprecated and should be zero.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.LocalSymsStripped
member.summary: Indicates COFF symbol table entries for local symbols have been removed. This flag is deprecated and should be zero.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.AggressiveWsTrim
member.summary: Indicates an aggressively trim working set is used. This flag is deprecated for Windows 2000 and later and must be zero.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.LargeAddressAware
member.summary: Indicates the application can handle larger than 2GB addresses.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.BytesReversedLo
member.summary: Indicates the file uses little endian. This flag is deprecated and should be zero.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.Machine32Bit
member.summary: Indicates the target machine is based on a 32-bit-word architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.DebugStripped
member.summary: Indicates debugging information is removed from the image file.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.RemovableRunFromSwap
member.summary: Indicates the image should be fully loaded and copied to the swap file if the image is on removable media.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.NetRunFromSwap
member.summary: Indicates the image should be fully loaded and copied to the swap file if the image is on a network media.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.System
member.summary: Indicates the image file is a system file, not a user program.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.Dll
member.summary: Indicates the image file is a dynamic-link library (DLL). Such files are considered executable files for almost all purposes, although they cannot be directly run.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.UpSystemOnly
member.summary: Indicates the file should be run only on a uniprocessor machine.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.Characteristics.BytesReversedHi
member.summary: Indicates the file uses big endian. This flag is deprecated and should be zero.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectory
member.summary: Represents a data directory header, consisting of the starting address, and the size.
member.remarks: This structure does not contain the actual contents of the data directory.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectory.DataDirectorySize
member.summary: Indicates the size of a single data directory header.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectory.FromReader(AsmResolver.IO.BinaryStreamReader@)
member.summary: Reads a single data directory at the current position of the provided input stream.
member.param: The input stream to read from.
member.returns: The data directory that was read.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectory.#ctor(System.UInt32,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Creates a new data directory header.
member.param: The starting virtual address (RVA) of the directory.
The size in bytes of the directory.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectory.VirtualAddress
member.summary: Gets the relative virtual address (RVA) of the directory.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectory.Size
member.summary: Gets the size of the directory.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectory.IsPresentInPE
member.summary: Gets a value indicating the data directory is present in the portable executable file.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectory.CreateForSegment(AsmResolver.ISegment)
member.summary: Creates a data directory descriptor for the provided segment.
member.param: The segment to create a data directory descriptor for.
member.returns: The data directory.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectory.Deconstruct(System.UInt32@,System.UInt32@)
member.summary: Deconstructs the data directory into a 2-tuple.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex
member.summary: Provides members describing the semantics of each data directory entry in an optional header.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.ExportDirectory
member.summary: Indicates the data directory entry containing exported symbols.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.ImportDirectory
member.summary: Indicates the directory entry containing a register of imported symbols.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.ResourceDirectory
member.summary: Indicates the directory entry referencing the root win32 resource directory.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.ExceptionDirectory
member.summary: Indicates the directory entry containing a register of exception handlers.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.CertificateDirectory
member.summary: Indicates the directory entry containing authentication signatures.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.BaseRelocationDirectory
member.summary: Indicates the directory entry containing base relocations that need to be applied to the image after loading.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.DebugDirectory
member.summary: Indicates the directory entry containing debug information.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.ArchitectureDirectory
member.summary: Reserved.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.GlobalPtrDirectory
member.summary: The RVA of the value to be stored in the global pointer register. The size member of this structure must be set to zero.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.TlsDirectory
member.summary: Indicates the directory entry containing the thread local storage (TLS) table.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.LoadConfigDirectory
member.summary: Indicates the directory entry containing the load configuration table.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.BoundImportDirectory
member.summary: Indicates the directory entry containing the bound import directory.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.IatDirectory
member.summary: Indicates the directory entry containing the import address table.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.DelayImportDescrDirectory
member.summary: Indicates the directory entry containing the delay import descriptor table.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.ClrDirectory
member.summary: Indicates the directory entry containing the CLR runtime headers.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex.ReservedDirectory
member.summary: Reserved.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.DllCharacteristics
member.summary: Provides members describing all possible flags that can be set in the field.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DllCharacteristics.HighEntropyVA
member.summary: Indicates the image can handle a high entropy 64-bit virtual address space.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DllCharacteristics.DynamicBase
member.summary: Indicates the DLL can be relocated at load time.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DllCharacteristics.ForceIntegrity
member.summary: Indicates Code Integrity checks are enforced.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DllCharacteristics.NxCompat
member.summary: Indicates the image is NX compatible.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DllCharacteristics.NoIsolation
member.summary: Indicates the image is isolation aware, but do not isolate the image.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DllCharacteristics.NoSeh
member.summary: Indicates the image does not use structured exception (SE) handling. No SE handler may be called in this image.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DllCharacteristics.NoBind
member.summary: Indicates the image must not be bound.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DllCharacteristics.AppContainer
member.summary: Indicates the image must execute in an AppContainer.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DllCharacteristics.WdmDriver
member.summary: Indicates the image is a WDM driver.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DllCharacteristics.ControlFLowGuard
member.summary: Indicates the image supports Control Flow Guard.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DllCharacteristics.TerminalServerAware
member.summary: Indicates the image is Terminal Server aware.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.DosHeader
member.summary: Represents the DOS header (also known as the MZ header) in the portable executable (PE) file format.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DosHeader.ValidPEMagic
member.summary: Indicates the magic constant that every DOS header should start with.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DosHeader.MinimalDosHeaderLength
member.summary: Indicates the minimal length of a valid DOS header in a portable executable file.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DosHeader.NextHeaderFieldOffset
member.summary: Indicates the offset of the e_flanew field in the DOS header.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.DosHeader.DefaultNewHeaderOffset
member.summary: Indicates the default value of the e_flanew field in the DOS header.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.DosHeader.FromReader(AsmResolver.IO.BinaryStreamReader@)
member.summary: Reads a DOS header structure at the current position of the provided input stream.
member.param: The input stream to read from.
member.returns: The read DOS header.
member.exception: Occurs when the input stream does not point to a valid DOS header.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.DosHeader.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new DOS header with the default contents.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.DosHeader.#ctor(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Creates a new DOS header with the provided contents.
member.param: The raw contents of the header.
member.exception: Occurs when the input data does not contain a valid DOS header.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.DosHeader.NextHeaderOffset
member.summary: Gets or sets the offset to the next header (NT header).

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.FileHeader
member.summary: Represents the COFF file header of a portable executable file.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.FileHeader.FileHeaderSize
member.summary: Indicates the static size of the COFF file header.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.FileHeader.FromReader(AsmResolver.IO.BinaryStreamReader@)
member.summary: Reads a COFF file header from an input stream.
member.returns: The read file header.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.FileHeader.Machine
member.summary: Gets or sets the machine the portable executable file is compiled for.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.FileHeader.NumberOfSections
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of sections defined in the portable executable file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.FileHeader.TimeDateStamp
member.summary: Gets or sets the time stamp of the portable executable file.
member.remarks: This timestamp is encoded as the number of seconds since 00:00 January 1, 1970.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.FileHeader.PointerToSymbolTable
member.summary: Gets or sets the absolute file offset to the symbol table.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.FileHeader.NumberOfSymbols
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of symbols defined in the portable executable file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.FileHeader.SizeOfOptionalHeader
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of the optional header.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.FileHeader.Characteristics
member.summary: Gets or sets the characteristics of the portable executable file.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType
member.summary: Provides members for each processor architecture that a portable executable (PE) file can encode for.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Unknown
member.summary: The content of this field is assumed to be applicable to any machine type.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.DotNetAppleOSOverride
member.summary: Provides a bitmask that is used to indicate the PE file is targeting Apple devices only.
member.remarks: This value is a bitmask and is not a valid machine type on its own. Furthermore, is only supposed to be used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.DotNetFreeBsdOSOverride
member.summary: Provides a bitmask that is used to indicate the PE file is targeting FreeBSD operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is a bitmask and is not a valid machine type on its own. Furthermore, is only supposed to be used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.DotNetLinuxOSOverride
member.summary: Provides a bitmask that is used to indicate the PE file is targeting Linux operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is a bitmask and is not a valid machine type on its own. Furthermore, is only supposed to be used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.DotNetNetBsdOSOverride
member.summary: Provides a bitmask that is used to indicate the PE file is targeting NetBSD operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is a bitmask and is not a valid machine type on its own. Furthermore, is only supposed to be used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.DotNetSunOSOverride
member.summary: Provides a bitmask that is used to indicate the PE file is targeting Sun operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is a bitmask and is not a valid machine type on its own. Furthermore, is only supposed to be used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Am33
member.summary: Indicates the Matsushita AM33 architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Amd64
member.summary: Indicates the x64 architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Amd64DotNetApple
member.summary: Indicates the x64 architecture, but specifically targets Apple devices.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Amd64DotNetFreeBsd
member.summary: Indicates the x64 architecture, but specifically targets FreeBSD operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Amd64DotNetLinux
member.summary: Indicates the x64 architecture, but specifically targets Linux operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Amd64DotNetNetBsd
member.summary: Indicates the x64 architecture, but specifically targets NetBSD operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Amd64DotNetSun
member.summary: Indicates the x64 architecture, but specifically targets SunOS operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Arm
member.summary: Indicates the ARM little endian architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.ArmNt
member.summary: Indicates the ARM thumb-2 little endian architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.ArmNtDotNetApple
member.summary: Indicates the ARM thumb-2 little endian architecture, but specifically targets Apple devices.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.ArmNtDotNetFreeBsd
member.summary: Indicates the ARM thumb-2 little endian architecture, but specifically targets FreeBSD operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.ArmNtDotNetLinux
member.summary: Indicates the ARM thumb-2 little endian architecture, but specifically targets Linux operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.ArmNtDotNetSun
member.summary: Indicates the ARM thumb-2 little endian architecture, but specifically targets SunOS operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.ArmNtDotNetNetBsd
member.summary: Indicates the ARM thumb-2 little endian architecture, but specifically targets NetBSD operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Arm64
member.summary: Indicates the ARM 64-bit little endian architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Arm64DotNetApple
member.summary: Indicates the ARM 64-bit little endian architecture, but specifically targets Apple devices.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Arm64DotNetFreeBsd
member.summary: Indicates the ARM 64-bit little endian architecture, but specifically targets FreeBSD operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Arm64DotNetLinux
member.summary: Indicates the ARM 64-bit little endian architecture, but specifically targets Linux operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Arm64DotNetNetBsd
member.summary: Indicates the ARM 64-bit little endian architecture, but specifically targets NetBSD operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Arm64DotNetSun
member.summary: Indicates the ARM 64-bit little endian architecturee, but specifically targets SunOS operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Ebc
member.summary: Indicates EFI byte code.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.I386
member.summary: Indicates the Intel 368 or compatible x86 (32-bit) architectures.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.I386DotNetApple
member.summary: Indicates the Intel 368 or compatible x86 (32-bit) architectures, but specifically targets Apple devices.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.I386DotNetFreeBsd
member.summary: Indicates the Intel 368 or compatible x86 (32-bit) architectures, but specifically targets FreeBSD operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.I386DotNetLinux
member.summary: Indicates the Intel 368 or compatible x86 (32-bit) architectures, but specifically targets Linux operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.I386DotNetNetBsd
member.summary: Indicates the Intel 368 or compatible x86 (32-bit) architectures, but specifically targets NetBSD operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.I386DotNetSun
member.summary: Indicates the Intel 368 or compatible x86 (32-bit) architectures, but specifically targets SunOS operating system derivatives.
member.remarks: This value is only used by .NET PE images. Reference: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/36364

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Ia64
member.summary: Indicates the Intel Itanium processor family.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.M32R
member.summary: Indicates the Mitsubishi M32R little endian architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Mips16
member.summary: Indicates the MIPS 16-bit architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.MipsFpu
member.summary: Indicates the MIPS architecture with FPU.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.MipsFpu16
member.summary: Indicates the MIPS 16-bit architecture with FPU.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.PowerPc
member.summary: Indicates the PowerPC little-endian architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.PowerPcFp
member.summary: Indicates the PowerPC little-endian architecture with floating point support.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.R4000
member.summary: Indicates the MIPS little endian architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Sh3
member.summary: Indicates the Hitachi SH3 architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Sh3Dsp
member.summary: Indicates the Hitachi SH3 DSP architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Sh4
member.summary: Indicates the Hitachi SH4 architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Sh5
member.summary: Indicates the Hitachi SH5 architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.Thumb
member.summary: Indicates the Thumb architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.WceMipsV2
member.summary: Indicates the MIPS little-endian WCE v2 architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.RiscV32
member.summary: Indicates the RISCV 32-bit architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.RiscV64
member.summary: Indicates the RISCV 64-bit architecture.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.MachineType.RiscV128
member.summary: Indicates the RISCV 128-bit architecture.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader
member.summary: Represents a 32-bit or 64-bit optional header in a portable executable (PE) file.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.OptionalHeaderSizeOfImageFieldOffset
member.summary: Indicates the offset of the SizeOfImage field in the optional header.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.OptionalHeader32SizeExcludingDataDirectories
member.summary: Indicates the static size of an optional header in a 32-bit portable executable file, excluding the data directory entries.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.OptionalHeader64SizeExcludingDataDirectories
member.summary: Indicates the static size of an optional header in a 64-bit portable executable file, excluding the data directory entries.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.DefaultNumberOfRvasAndSizes
member.summary: Indicates the default number of data directory entries in an optional header.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.FromReader(AsmResolver.IO.BinaryStreamReader@,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Reads an optional header from the provided input stream.
member.param: Indicates whether the number of data directories should be ignored and should be used instead.
member.returns: The optional header.
member.exception: Occurs when the input stream is malformed.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.Magic
member.summary: Gets or sets the magic optional header signature, determining whether the image is a PE32 (32-bit) or a PE32+ (64-bit) image.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.MajorLinkerVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the major linker version used to link the portable executable (PE) file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.MinorLinkerVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the minor linker version used to link the portable executable (PE) file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.SizeOfCode
member.summary: Gets or sets the total amount of bytes the code sections consist of.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.SizeOfInitializedData
member.summary: Gets or sets the total amount of bytes the initialized data sections consist of.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.SizeOfUninitializedData
member.summary: Gets or sets the total amount of bytes the uninitialized data sections consist of.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.AddressOfEntryPoint
member.summary: Gets or sets the relative virtual address to the entry point of the portable executable (PE) file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.BaseOfCode
member.summary: Gets or sets the relative virtual address (RVA) to the beginning of the code section, when loaded into memory.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.BaseOfData
member.summary: Gets or sets the relative virtual address to the begin of the data section, when loaded into memory.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.ImageBase
member.summary: Gets or sets the preferred address of the first byte of the image when loaded into memory. Must be a multiple of 64,000.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.SectionAlignment
member.summary: Gets or sets the alignment of the sections when loaded into memory. Must be greater or equal to . Default is the page size for the architecture.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.FileAlignment
member.summary: Gets or sets the alignment of the raw data of sections in the image file. Must be a power of 2 between 512 and 64,000.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.MajorOperatingSystemVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum major version of the operating system required to run the portable executable (PE) file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.MinorOperatingSystemVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum minor version of the operating system required to run the portable executable (PE) file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.MajorImageVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the major image version.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.MinorImageVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the minor image version.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.MajorSubsystemVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the major version of the subsystem.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.MinorSubsystemVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the minor version of the subsystem.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.Win32VersionValue
member.summary: Reserved, should be zero.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.SizeOfImage
member.summary: Gets or sets the size in bytes of the portable executable (PE) file, including all headers. Must be a multiple of .

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.SizeOfHeaders
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of the headers of the portable executable (PE) file, including the DOS-, PE- and section headers, rounded to .

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.CheckSum
member.summary: Gets or sets the checksum of the portable executable (PE) file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.SubSystem
member.summary: Gets or sets the subsystem to use when running the portable executable (PE) file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.DllCharacteristics
member.summary: Gets or sets the dynamic linked library characteristics of the portable executable (PE) file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.SizeOfStackReserve
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of the stack to reserve.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.SizeOfStackCommit
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of the stack to commit.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.SizeOfHeapReserve
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of the heap to reserve.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.SizeOfHeapCommit
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of the heap to commit.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.LoaderFlags
member.summary: Obsolete.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.NumberOfRvaAndSizes
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of data directory headers defined in the optional header.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.DataDirectories
member.summary: Gets the data directory headers defined in the optional header.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.GetDataDirectory(AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex)
member.summary: Gets a data directory by its index.
member.returns: The data directory entry.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.SetDataDirectory(AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex,AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectory)
member.summary: Sets a data directory by its index.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.SetDataDirectory(AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectoryIndex,AsmResolver.ISegment)
member.summary: Maps a segment to a data directory.
member.param: The contents of the data directory, or null to indicate an absence of the directory.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader.EnsureDataDirectoryCount(System.Int32)
member.summary: Ensures the provided number of data directories are present in .

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeaderMagic
member.summary: Provides valid values for the optional header magic.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeaderMagic.PE32
member.summary: Indicates the binary contains a 32-bit portable executable file.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeaderMagic.PE32Plus
member.summary: Indicates the binary contains a 64-bit portable executable file.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeaderMagic.PE64
member.summary: Indicates the binary contains a 64-bit portable executable file.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile
member.summary: Models a file using the portable executable (PE) file format. It provides access to various PE headers, as well as the raw contents of each section present in the file.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.ValidPESignature
member.summary: Indicates a valid NT header signature.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new empty portable executable file.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.#ctor(AsmResolver.PE.File.DosHeader,AsmResolver.PE.File.FileHeader,AsmResolver.PE.File.OptionalHeader)
member.summary: Creates a new portable executable file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.FilePath
member.summary: When this PE file was read from the disk, gets the file path to the PE file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.DosHeader
member.summary: Gets or sets the DOS header of the PE file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.FileHeader
member.summary: Gets or sets the COFF file header of the portable executable (PE) file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.OptionalHeader
member.summary: Gets or sets the optional header of the portable executable (PE) file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.Sections
member.summary: Gets a collection of sections present in the portable executable (PE) file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.MappingMode
member.summary: Gets a value indicating the mapping mode of the PE file. If the PE file is in its mapped form, then every offset of all segments in the PE file will be equal to the physical memory address. If the file is in its unmapped form, the offsets will be equal to the file offset.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.ExtraSectionData
member.summary: Gets or sets the padding data in between the last section header and the first section.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.EofData
member.summary: Gets or sets the data appended to the end of the file (EoF), if available.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.FromFile(System.String)
member.summary: Reads an unmapped PE file from the disk.
member.param: The file path to the PE file.
member.returns: The PE file that was read.
member.exception: Occurs when the file does not follow the PE file format.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.FromFile(AsmResolver.IO.IInputFile)
member.summary: Reads an unmapped PE file.
member.returns: The PE file that was read.
member.exception: Occurs when the file does not follow the PE file format.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.FromBytes(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Reads an unmapped PE file from memory.
member.param: The raw bytes representing the contents of the PE file to read.
member.returns: The PE file that was read.
member.exception: Occurs when the file does not follow the PE file format.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.FromStream(System.IO.Stream)
member.summary: Reads an unmapped PE file from memory.
member.param: The raw stream representing the contents of the PE file to read.
member.returns: The PE file that was read.
member.exception: Occurs when the file does not follow the PE file format.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.FromModuleBaseAddress(System.IntPtr,AsmResolver.PE.File.PEMappingMode)
member.summary: Reads a PE file starting at the provided module base address (HINSTANCE).
member.param: The HINSTANCE or base address of the module.
Indicates how the input PE file is mapped.
member.returns: The PE file that was read.
member.exception: Occurs when the file does not follow the PE file format.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.FromDataSource(AsmResolver.IO.IDataSource,AsmResolver.PE.File.PEMappingMode)
member.summary: Reads a PE file from the provided data source.
member.param: The data source to read from.
Indicates how the input PE file is mapped.
member.returns: The PE file that was read.
member.exception: Occurs when the file does not follow the PE file format.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.FromReader(AsmResolver.IO.BinaryStreamReader@,AsmResolver.PE.File.PEMappingMode)
member.summary: Reads a PE file from the provided input stream.
member.param: The input stream to read from.
Indicates how the input PE file is mapped.
member.returns: The PE file that was read.
member.exception: Occurs when the file does not follow the PE file format.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.GetSectionContainingOffset(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Finds the section containing the provided file offset.
member.returns: The section containing the file offset.
member.exception: Occurs when the file offset does not fall within any of the sections.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.TryGetSectionContainingOffset(System.UInt64,AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection@)
member.summary: Attempts to find the section containing the provided file offset.
member.returns: true if the section was found, false otherwise.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.GetSectionContainingRva(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Finds the section containing the provided virtual address.
member.returns: The section containing the virtual address.
member.exception: Occurs when the virtual address does not fall within any of the sections.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.TryGetSectionContainingRva(System.UInt32,AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection@)
member.summary: Attempts to find the section containing the provided virtual address.
member.returns: true if the section was found, false otherwise.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.CreateDataDirectoryReader(AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectory)
member.summary: Obtains a reader that spans the provided data directory.
member.returns: The reader.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.TryCreateDataDirectoryReader(AsmResolver.PE.File.DataDirectory,AsmResolver.IO.BinaryStreamReader@)
member.summary: Attempts to create a reader that spans the provided data directory.
member.returns: true if the reader was created successfully, false otherwise.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.CreateReaderAtFileOffset(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Creates a new reader at the provided file offset.
member.param: The file offset to start reading at.
member.returns: The reader.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.TryCreateReaderAtFileOffset(System.UInt64,AsmResolver.IO.BinaryStreamReader@)
member.summary: Attempts to create a new reader at the provided file offset.
member.param: The file offset to start reading at.
member.returns: true if the reader was created successfully, false otherwise.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.CreateReaderAtFileOffset(System.UInt64,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Creates a new reader of a chunk of data at the provided file offset.
member.param: The file offset to start reading at.
The number of bytes in the chunk.
member.returns: The reader.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.TryCreateReaderAtFileOffset(System.UInt64,System.UInt32,AsmResolver.IO.BinaryStreamReader@)
member.summary: Attempts to create a new reader of a chunk of data at the provided file offset.
member.param: The file offset to start reading at.
The number of bytes in the chunk.
member.returns: true if the reader was created successfully, false otherwise.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.CreateReaderAtRva(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Creates a new reader at the provided virtual address.
member.param: The virtual address to start reading at.
member.returns: The reader.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.TryCreateReaderAtRva(System.UInt32,AsmResolver.IO.BinaryStreamReader@)
member.summary: Attempts to create a new reader at the provided virtual address.
member.param: The virtual address to start reading at.
member.returns: true if the reader was created successfully, false otherwise.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.CreateReaderAtRva(System.UInt32,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Creates a new reader of a chunk of data at the provided virtual address.
member.param: The virtual address to start reading at.
The number of bytes in the chunk.
member.returns: The reader.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.TryCreateReaderAtRva(System.UInt32,System.UInt32,AsmResolver.IO.BinaryStreamReader@)
member.summary: Attempts to create a new reader of a chunk of data at the provided virtual address.
member.param: The virtual address to start reading at.
The number of bytes in the chunk.
member.returns: true if the reader was created successfully, false otherwise.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.UpdateHeaders
member.summary: Recomputes file offsets and sizes in the file, optional and section headers.
member.remarks:
Affected fields in the file header include:
SizeOfOptionalHeader
Affected fields in the optional header include:
SizeOfHeaders SizeOfImage Data directories
Affected fields in the section header include:
VirtualAddress VirtualSize PointerToRawData SizeOfRawData

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.AlignSections
member.summary: Aligns all sections according to the file and section alignment properties in the optional header.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.AlignDataDirectoryEntries(System.Collections.Generic.IList{AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader})
member.summary: Aligns all data directories' virtual address according to the section header's ones.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.Write(System.String)
member.summary: Writes the PE file to a file on the disk.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.Write(System.IO.Stream)
member.summary: Writes the PE file to the provided output stream.
member.param: The output stream to write to.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.Write(AsmResolver.IO.BinaryStreamWriter)
member.summary: Writes the PE file to the provided output stream.
member.param: The output stream to write to.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.GetSections
member.summary: Obtains the sections in the portable executable file.
member.returns: The section.
member.remarks: This method is called upon the initialization of the property.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.GetExtraSectionData
member.summary: Obtains the padding data in between the last section header and the first section.
member.returns: The extra padding data.
member.remarks: This method is called upon the initialization of the property.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile.GetEofData
member.summary: Obtains any data appended to the end of the file (EoF).
member.returns: The extra data.
member.remarks: This method is called upon the initialization of the property.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEMappingMode
member.summary: Provides members for describing whether a portable executable file is in its mapped or unmapped form.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEMappingMode.Unmapped
member.summary: Indicates the portable executable is in its unmapped form. Every offset in the portable executable refers to a physical file offset as it would appear on disk.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.PEMappingMode.Mapped
member.summary: Indicates the portable executable is in its mapped form. Every offset in the portable executable refers to an absolute memory address.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection
member.summary: Represents a single section in a portable executable (PE) file.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.#ctor(AsmResolver.Utf8String,AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags)
member.summary: Creates a new empty section.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.#ctor(AsmResolver.Utf8String,AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags,AsmResolver.ISegment)
member.summary: Creates a new empty section.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.#ctor(AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection)
member.summary: Copy a new section.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.#ctor(AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader,AsmResolver.ISegment)
member.summary: Creates a new section with the provided contents.
member.param: The header to associate to the section.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.ContainingFile
member.summary: Gets the portable executable file this section is part of.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the section.
member.remarks: The name of the section is a UTF-8 string that can be no longer than 8 characters long.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.Characteristics
member.summary: Gets or sets the characteristics of the section.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.IsContentCode
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the section contains executable code.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.IsContentInitializedData
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the section contains initialized data.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.IsContentUninitializedData
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the section contains uninitialized data.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.IsMemoryDiscardable
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the section can be discarded as needed.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.IsMemoryNotCached
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the section cannot be cached.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.IsMemoryNotPaged
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the section is not pageable.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.IsMemoryShared
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the section can be shared in memory.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.IsMemoryExecute
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the section can be executed as code.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.IsMemoryRead
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the section can be read.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.IsMemoryWrite
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating the section can be written.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.Contents
member.summary: Gets or sets the contents of the section.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.IsReadable
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the section is readable using a binary stream reader.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.CreateHeader
member.summary: Creates a new header for this section.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.ContainsFileOffset(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Determines whether the provided file offset falls within the section that the header describes.
member.returns: true if the file offset falls within the section, false otherwise.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESection.ContainsRva(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Determines whether the provided virtual address falls within the section that the header describes.
member.returns: true if the virtual address falls within the section, false otherwise.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESectionCollection
member.summary: Represents a collection of sections stored in a portable executable file.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESectionCollection.#ctor(AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the class.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESectionCollection.Owner
member.summary: Gets the PE file containing the sections.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESegmentReference
member.summary: Represents a reference to a segment of a PE file.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESegmentReference.#ctor(AsmResolver.PE.File.PEFile,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Creates a new PE reference.
member.param: The virtual address of the segment.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESegmentReference.IsValidAddress
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the reference points to a valid section within the PE file.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.PESegmentReference.PointsToPhysicalData
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the reference points to a valid physical section within the PE file.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags
member.summary: Provides members describing all possible flags that can be assigned to a section.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.TypeDsect
member.summary: Reserved for future use.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.TypeNoLoad
member.summary: Reserved for future use.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.TypeGroup
member.summary: Reserved for future use.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.TypeNoPadded
member.summary: The section should not be padded to the next boundary. This flag is obsolete and is replaced by Align1Bytes. This is valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.TypeCopy
member.summary: Reserved for future use.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.ContentCode
member.summary: The section contains executable code.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.ContentInitializedData
member.summary: The section contains initialized data.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.ContentUninitializedData
member.summary: The section contains uninitialized data.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.LinkOther
member.summary: Reserved for future use.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.LinkInfo
member.summary: The section contains comments or other information. The .drectve section has this type. This is valid for object files only.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.TypeOver
member.summary: Reserved for future use.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.LinkRemove
member.summary: The section will not become part of the image. This is valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.LinkComDat
member.summary: The section contains COMDAT data. For more information, see section 5.5.6, COMDAT Sections (Object Only). This is valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.NoDeferSpecExceptions
member.summary: Reset speculative exceptions handling bits in the TLB entries for this section.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.RelativeGp
member.summary: The section contains data referenced through the global pointer (GP).

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.MemPurgeable
member.summary: Reserved for future use.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Memory16Bit
member.summary: Reserved for future use.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.MemoryLocked
member.summary: Reserved for future use.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.MemoryPreload
member.summary: Reserved for future use.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align1Bytes
member.summary: Align data on a 1-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align2Bytes
member.summary: Align data on a 2-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align4Bytes
member.summary: Align data on a 4-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align8Bytes
member.summary: Align data on an 8-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align16Bytes
member.summary: Align data on a 16-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align32Bytes
member.summary: Align data on a 32-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align64Bytes
member.summary: Align data on a 64-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align128Bytes
member.summary: Align data on a 128-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align256Bytes
member.summary: Align data on a 256-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align512Bytes
member.summary: Align data on a 512-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align1024Bytes
member.summary: Align data on a 1024-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align2048Bytes
member.summary: Align data on a 2048-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align4096Bytes
member.summary: Align data on a 4096-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.Align8192Bytes
member.summary: Align data on an 8192-byte boundary. Valid only for object files.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.LinkExtendedRelocationOverflow
member.summary: The section contains extended relocations.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.MemoryDiscardable
member.summary: The section can be discarded as needed.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.MemoryNotCached
member.summary: The section cannot be cached.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.MemoryNotPaged
member.summary: The section is not pageable.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.MemoryShared
member.summary: The section can be shared in memory.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.MemoryExecute
member.summary: The section can be executed as code.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.MemoryRead
member.summary: The section can be read.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags.MemoryWrite
member.summary: The section can be written.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader
member.summary: Represents a single section header in the portable executable (PE) file format.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.SectionHeaderSize
member.summary: Indicates the static size of a single section header.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.#ctor(AsmResolver.Utf8String,AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionFlags)
member.summary: Creates a new section header with the provided name.
member.param: The name of the new section.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.#ctor(AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader)
member.summary: Creates a copy of the provided section header.
member.param: The section header to base information on.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the section.
member.remarks: The name of the section is a UTF-8 string that can be no longer than 8 characters long.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.VirtualSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the total number of bytes the section consists of when it is loaded into memory.
member.remarks: If the value is greater than , it is zero-padded when loaded into memory.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.VirtualAddress
member.summary: Gets or sets the virtual address (relative to the image base) of the section.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.SizeOfRawData
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of bytes the section consists of on disk.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.PointerToRawData
member.summary: Gets or sets the file offset to the beginning of the section on disk.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.PointerToRelocations
member.summary: Gets or sets the file offset to the beginning of the relocations table for the section.
member.remarks: This field is set to zero in a normal PE image.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.PointerToLineNumbers
member.summary: Gets or sets the file offset to the beginning of the line numbers table for the section.
member.remarks: This field is set to zero in a normal PE image.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.NumberOfRelocations
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of relocations in the relocation table of the section.
member.remarks: This field is set to zero for executable files.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.NumberOfLineNumbers
member.summary: Gets or sets the total amount of line numbers table referenced by for the section.
member.remarks: This field is not used in a normal PE image.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.Characteristics
member.summary: Gets or sets the characteristics of the section.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.FromReader(AsmResolver.IO.BinaryStreamReader@)
member.summary: Reads a single section header at the current position of the provided input stream.
member.param: The input stream to read from.
member.returns: The section header that was read.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.ContainsFileOffset(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Determines whether the provided file offset falls within the section that the header describes.
member.returns: true if the file offset falls within the section, false otherwise.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.SectionHeader.ContainsRva(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Determines whether the provided virtual address falls within the section that the header describes.
member.returns: true if the virtual address falls within the section, false otherwise.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.SerializedPEFile
member.summary: Provides an implementation of a PE image that gets its data from an input stream.

member.name: M:AsmResolver.PE.File.SerializedPEFile.#ctor(AsmResolver.IO.BinaryStreamReader@,AsmResolver.PE.File.PEMappingMode)
member.summary: Reads a PE file from an input stream.
member.param: Indicates how the input PE file is mapped.
member.exception: Occurs when the input stream is malformed.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem
member.summary: Provides members describing all supported subsystems that a portable executable can run in.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem.Unknown
member.summary: Indicates an unknown subsystem.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem.Native
member.summary: Indicates device drivers and native Windows processes.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem.WindowsGui
member.summary: Indicates the Windows graphical user interface subsystem.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem.WindowsCui
member.summary: Indicates the Windows character subsystem.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem.PosixCui
member.summary: Indicates the POSIX character subsystem.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem.NativeWindows
member.summary: Indicates a native Win9x driver.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem.WindowsCeGui
member.summary: Indicates a Windows CE graphical user interface application.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem.EfiApplication
member.summary: Indicates an extensible firmware interface application.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem.EfiBootServiceDriver
member.summary: Indicates an extensible firmware interface driver with boot services.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem.EfiRuntime
member.summary: Indicates an extensible firmware interface driver with run-time services.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem.EfiRom
member.summary: Indicates an extensible firmware interface ROM image.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem.Xbox
member.summary: Indicates an XBOX application.

member.name: F:AsmResolver.PE.File.SubSystem.WindowsBootApplication
member.summary: Indicates a Windows boot application.

member.name: T:AsmResolver.LazyPropertyAttribute
member.summary: Specifies an auto property is lazily initialized and that this implementation is provided by a source generator
member.remarks:
The source generator generates for every property X marked with this attribute the following:
A private field _x to store the value of the property
A private constant XMask specifying the bit mask within the initialization vector _initialized
A call to a parameterless method GetX()
In case is set, the source generator will also generate code to automatically test and update exclusive ownership of the property's value.

member.name: P:AsmResolver.LazyPropertyAttribute.OwnerProperty
member.summary: When non-null, gets the name of the property that reflects the owner of the marked property's value.
member.remarks: Use this to indicate the value of the property is exclusively owned by at most one instance of the enclosing class.
